item.title: Hong Kong’s New World Clan Bets on Property Rebound for Lifeline
item.description: Unwilling to cede control over New World Development Co., Hong Kong’s billionaire Cheng family is now betting on the revival of the city’s property market and mulling options like a public share sale to meet the embattled developer’s debt obligations.

item.title: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang Urges AI Leaders to Avoid Fearmongering
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said tech leaders need to be careful not to scare people about artificial intelligence, in response to a question about how Anthropic PBC could have better handled messy contract negotiations with the Pentagon.

item.title: Firefly Revenue Beats Estimates as Firm Returns Alpha To Flight
item.description: Firefly Aerospace Inc. posted record annual revenue and a narrower-than-expected quarterly loss, capping a 2025 marked by a moon landing and public offering as well as explosive mishaps with its core rocket.

item.title: After Qatar LNG Plant Outage More Buyers Turn to US Suppliers
item.description: An increasing number of purchasers and importers scrambling to secure liquefied natural gas turned to the US on Thursday after the latest attack on Qatar’s massive LNG complex further strained global supplies of the fuel.

item.title: Blackstone’s $83 Billion BCRED Is Selling New Private Credit CLO
item.description: Blackstone Inc.’s flagship private credit fund is planning to sell bonds backed by a broad swathe of its $82.5 billion of assets, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Sheinbaum Proposes Bill to Bolster Infrastructure Investment
item.description: Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum sent a bill to Congress aimed at promoting public and private investment in strategic infrastructure, at a time when her government is pushing to lure capital to stimulate sluggish economic growth.

item.title: Iran War Ripples Are Driving Up Prices for Plastics Ingredient
item.description: US producers of polyethylene — a common plastic used in everything from packaging to containers — are buying more of a key ingredient, ethylene, in a sign that manufacturers are working to take advantage of export opportunities as global supplies tighten.

item.title: Kalshi Gets $1 Billion in New Funding at $22 Billion Valuation
item.description: Kalshi Inc. has raised more than $1 billion at a valuation of $22 billion in a new financing round, according to a person familiar with the situation.

item.title: Silicon Valley Confronts AI’s Big PR Problem
item.description: Industry insiders acknowledge that many in the US have negative feelings about the technology.

item.title: Bezos Seeks $100 Billion to Use AI in Manufacturing, WSJ Says
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. founder Jeff Bezos is in talks to raise $100 billion for a fund to acquire manufacturing companies and infuse them with artificial intelligence, the Wall Street Journal reported.

item.title: 威胁美国经济的巨大鸿沟
item.description: It takes just minutes to destroy energy infrastructure and it will takes months to rebuild it.

item.title: The Texas Stock Exchange Lures Leadership From Nasdaq, NYSE as it Targets Listings
item.description: The Texas Stock Exchange, an upstart Dallas-based bourse backed by Michael Dell, BlackRock and some of the world’s biggest asset managers, has poached executives from Nasdaq Inc. and the New York Stock Exchange, as it seeks to bring stock listings to the Lone Star State.

item.title: Google Begins Testing Gemini Mac App to Match ChatGPT and Claude
item.description: Google is ramping up development of a dedicated Gemini AI app for Apple Inc.’s Mac computer lineup, looking to step up competition with OpenAI and Anthropic PBC.

item.title: SXSW Is Being Swallowed By the Creator Economy
item.description: Five Takeaways From the Annual Texas Gathering of Cultural Heavyweights

item.title: Position Wash-Outs in Few Manic Minutes Spurred Fed Hike Pricing
item.description: Aggressive price action early Thursday across the cash and futures markets covering US rates, and the subsequent bounce-back across many assets showed footprints of position flush-outs — exacerbating the price action over a manic few minutes around 8:30am New York time.

item.title: Dollar’s Oil-Fueled Rally Is Derailed By Surging Global Yields
item.description: The dollar fell against most of its major peers Thursday after key central banks flagged the risk of an inflationary shock amid the intensifying Middle East conflict.

item.title: Goldman M&A Head Says Buyers Are Keeping ‘Eyes on the Sunset’
item.description: Wall Street is in the middle of a dealmaking cycle that promises plenty more transformational mergers, according to the head of M&A at Goldman Sachs Group Inc., who said companies are now well-versed in navigating volatility.

item.title: Wall Street Faces a $5.7 Trillion Triple-Witching Jolt on Friday
item.description: Wall Street equities traders are bracing for an unusually large tally of options expiring on Friday, which risks injecting even more volatility into a market that’s seen weeks of turbulence amid the raging Mideast conflict.

item.title: First Foreign Tankers Tapped for US Transit Since Trump Waiver
item.description: At least two foreign-flagged fuel tankers have been tentatively booked to travel between US ports, marking the first such sailings in years after President Donald Trump temporarily waived a century-old shipping mandate in a bid to tame energy inflation.

item.title: School Choice Programs Get Nod of Approval in Illinois Primaries
item.description: Democratic governors across the US are facing pressure on whether to back President Donald Trump’s controversial new federal school choice program, a dilemma illustrated by this week’s Illinois primary election.

item.title: Banks Amp Up Pressure on Brazil’s Cosan for Better Raízen Deal
item.description: Some Brazilian banks with exposure to Raízen SA are pushing parent Cosan SA and its adviser BTG Pactual, seeking better terms in the sugar and ethanol company’s debt restructuring, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Costco Lines, Canceled Trips, Fuel Rewards: Americans Hunt for Cheap Gas
item.description: Americans are looking for ways to cut back as gas prices surge to nearly $4 a gallon due to the Iran war.

item.title: White House Not Planning to Ban Oil, Gas Exports, Official Says
item.description: The White House is not considering putting restrictions on the export of oil and gas, a Trump administration official said Thursday, following a meeting between Vice President JD Vance and oil executives.

item.title: Canada Benchmark Set to Erase Gains For Year Amid Gold Meltdown
item.description: The Iran war has weakened gold prices, with the commodity falling for a seventh straight session on Thursday as the conflict escalated. The drop means Canada’s stock index, which is heavily tilted to the metals sector, is set to give up its 2026 gains.

item.title: Oaktree-Backed UK Hospitality Group RedCat Seeks Fresh Funding
item.description: RedCat, the British hospitality group started by former Greene King Plc boss Rooney Anand, is exploring options including bringing in fresh investment to fund expansion, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Bank of England Hits Reverse as White House Drives UK Rate Moves
item.description: Donald Trump and the conflict in the Middle East are now determining monetary policy in the UK. That’s effectively the assessment of the Bank of England, which warned that Britons will be paying the price.

item.title: US Allies See No Way of Opening Hormuz Strait During War
item.description: Europe’s biggest naval powers don’t see a way of reopening the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping before the Iran war subsides.

item.title: Citi Gave £100 Million Credit Line to Lender Backed by MFS’ Raja
item.description: Citigroup Inc. provided a £100 million ($134 million) funding line to Interbridge Mortgages, another fast-growing UK lender backed by Paresh Raja, just as his Market Financial Solutions Ltd. was hurtling toward collapse.

item.title: British Columbia Debt Downgraded Again on ‘Entrenched’ Deficit
item.description: British Columbia was downgraded by Moody’s Ratings, which reiterated its negative outlook, the latest in a string of downgrades for Canada’s third-most populous province as its fiscal position worsens amid macroeconomic pressure.

item.title: SEC Targets Auditing ‘Bad Actors’ With New Enforcement Team
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission is creating a new enforcement team to target “bad actors” in the auditing profession after the agency cut the budget of the independent board that traditionally polices those responsible for vetting company financial statements.
